source_files: Check_List_BOM.xlsx (Inventor BOM export, fully structured, in-progress)
fixture_meta.notes: No other documentation provided beyond these two files. parts[] reflects raw Inventor export as-is, not yet normalized to PPM schema (parent_id/assembly_level/part_operations). Canonical part images (492, deduplicated by part_number) live in Supabase Storage, not in this repo -- see image_storage section below. Row-level duplicate images (643 total, same image repeated across duplicate-PN rows) were not uploaded, only canonical-per-part-number images.

part_number_pattern_categories.stirg_c_number_inventor_path: C###### optionally chained with .N or _N Inventor occurrence-path suffixes; same physical part can repeat across multiple paths
part_number_pattern_categories.raw_catalog_numeric: bare integer, no prefix — looks like an unassigned Inventor default number
part_number_pattern_categories.structured_project_number: e.g. 003.A00.S00.P000 — zone-style code, only seen on one assembly (Pallet_Frame) in this BOM
part_number_pattern_categories.empty: no Part_Number at all, Description present instead
part_number_pattern_categories.other: mostly C-number variants with a trailing underscore (Inventor formatting artifact)

findings_for_cross_analysis: Item hierarchy depth ranges 1-7 (depth distribution: 1:89, 2:109, 3:110, 4:214, 5:104, 6:16, 7:1). This exceeds the documented three-level BOM maximum (D-96: 0=assembly/1=sub-assembly/2=part). No collapsing rule from raw Inventor nesting depth to PPM's 3-tier assembly_level exists yet.

492 unique part numbers across 643 rows (94 part numbers repeat across 2+ rows). Consistent with D-97 (same PN in multiple assemblies = one row per WO instance, qty summed) but confirms real-world frequency is high (~15% of rows are repeats) for this supplier's BOM style.

Four distinct part_number formats coexist in a single BOM file (see schema_notes). 25 rows have no part number at all and 17 have a bare unassigned-looking integer — both are auto-assign (D-18) / generic-flag (D-119) candidates, suggesting the auto-assign and flagging logic needs to handle 'numeric-only PN' as its own detection case, not just 'empty'.

GST's actual 11 operation/status columns do not match the architecture.md 9-segment pipeline: Laser cutting and Cut to length are tracked as two separate operations, and powder coating has 3 timestamp sub-fields (sent/should-arrive/arrived) rather than a single status. All values are binary Done/blank at source — no In Progress state exists in this org's current process. Supports the org_operations per-org customization model (D-121) over any fixed global pipeline.

643 embedded 180x180 JPEG thumbnails extracted, one per row, deduplicated to 492 canonical images by first-occurrence part number (matches part_reference_photos design, D-84). Canonical selection is first-occurrence-in-file-order; rows sharing a part number were not checked against each other for image consistency.

Offer_GST.xlsx procurement sheet contains 23 real line items (all manufacturer/distributor bought-in parts, all Make_or_Buy='Buy') out of 810 allocated rows. The sheet has columns for in-house ('Blechteile') production cost/markup but none are populated here -- in-house costing for this WO is not present in either file.

Offer_GST.xlsx procurement columns map reasonably well to procurement-tracking.md fields: lead_time_weeks, sent/expected/actual dates, and an explicit 'Latest order date' + 'Days left to Order' pair that closely mirrors the spec's last_order_date/alert concept.

Offer_GST.xlsx 'Suppliers' sheet has 93 full supplier records (name, group, distributor, contact, lead time, payment terms, incoterm, full address, comment) with no corresponding table in the current schema -- only a free-text supplier field on procurement exists today. This is a real master-data gap if structured supplier tracking is wanted.

image_storage.location: Supabase Storage (bucket/path convention pending confirmation with Voja)
image_storage.note: filenames in parts[].image_file_canonical correspond to the object name once uploaded under this path pattern

suppliers_note: Supplier directory (93 records: name, contact, email, phone, address) withheld from this public-repo copy pending decision on where supplier contact data should live -- see open question raised with Voja.
